properties.remark: TWO WATERSPOUTS REPORTED LASTING BETWEEN 130 AND 141 PM EST BY THE PUBLIC. SPOUTS WERE MEDIUM IN SIZE...HAD VISIBLE SPRAY RING...AND LOCATED NEAR A RAIN SHAFT.

AT APPROXIMATELY 638 AM EST A WATERSPOUT CAME ASHORE ON COCO PLUM DRIVE IN MARATHON. THIS TORNADO CAUSED MINOR CANOPY DAMAGE TO A BUSINESS IN THE AREA. COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGERS ARE ASSESSING THE DAMAGE AT THIS TIME. MAGNITUDE HAS NOT BEEN DETERMINED YET.
A THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 44 KNOTS...50 MPH WAS MEASURED AT THE WEATHERFLOW MESONET OBSERVATION SITE AT SMITH SHOAL LIGHT.

A LARGE WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED OVER HAWK CHANNEL S OF DUCK KEY BY THE PUBLIC AT APPROXIMATELY 257 PM EST. THE REPORT WAS RELAYED BY THE MONROE COUNTY SHERIFFS OFFICE. THIS SPOUT MOVED E...AND EITHER DISSIPATED OR BECAME OBSCURED BY RAIN. A LARGE WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED AGAIN 5 TO 7 MILES S OF ISLAMORADA...THIS TIME BY A TRAINED SPOTTER. THE PARENT THUNDERSTORM WAS THE SAME ONE ASSOCIATED WITH THE SPOUT OBSERVED S OF DUCK KEY. THE SPOUT LASTED ANOTHER 10 MINUTES BEFORE BECOMING OBSCURED BY RAIN.
LARGE WATERSPOUT TRAVELING EAST OBSERVED BY TRAINED SPOTTER LASTING MORE THAN 10 MINUTES.

AT THE TIME OF THE CALL, THE WATERSPOUT APPEARED TO BE IN ITS DISSIPATION STAGE, TAKING ON AT LEAST A 45 DEGREE TILT, AND ONLY VISIBLE ABOUT A QUARTER OF THE WAY DOWN FROM THE CLOUD BASE. A SPRAY RING WAS NOT VISIBLE AS THE SEA SURFACE WAS OBSTRUCTED.

AN OUTFLOW FROM A MARINE THUNDERSTORM GENERATED A WIND GUST OF 40 MPH OR 35 KNOTS AT THE KEY WEST INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT.

A WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED BY A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC ON SOCIAL MEDIA. WATERSPOUT WAS ABOUT 6 MILES SOUTH OF KEY LARGO AND JUST OFFSHORE OF TAVERNIER ONTHE ATLANTIC SIDE. WATERSPOUT WAS EXTENDING 2/3 OF THE WAY DOWN FROM THE CLOUD BASE. SPRAY RING WAS INDETERMINANT AT THE TIME OF THE REPORT AND LASTED EIGHT MINUTES.

CHARTER BOAT REPORTED MULTIPLE WATERSPOUTS VIA SOCIAL MEDIA PICTURE OCCURING SHORTLY AFTER 12PM. LOCATION OF THE WATERSPOUTS WERE ABOUT 4 MILES SOUTH SOUTHEAST OF BOCA GRANDE. SPRAY RING WAS VISIBLE IN THE SHARED PHOTOGRAPH.

ON-DUTY NWS METEOROLOGIST OBSERVED A WATERSPOUT APPROXIMATELY 7 MILES WEST OF OLD TOWN KEY WEST...NEAR COTTRELL KEY. THE WATERSPOUT WAS EXTENDING DOWN FROM THE CLOUD BASE, ADJACENT TO A VISIBLE RAINSHAFT. SPRAY RING WAS NOT OBSERVED AS THE SEA SURFACE WAS OBSTRUCTED. WATERSPOUT WAS VIEWABLE FOR APPROXIMATELY 10 MINUTES BEFORE FADING BEHIND THE RAINSHAFT AND CLOUDS.
WATERSPOUT REPORTED BY MCSO AT 430 PM TODAY.

AN NWS METEOROLOGIST REPORTED A WATERSPOUT APPROXIMATELY 8 MILES NORTH OF STOCK ISLAND. AT THE TIME OF THE REPORT, THE WATERSPOUT APPEARED FULLY DEVELOPED BUT A SPRAY RING WAS NOT VISIBLE DUE TO AN OBSTRUCTED VIEW. AFTER RECEIVING THE REPORT, THE WATERSPOUT DISSIPATED 10 MINUTES LATER.

PUBLIC RELAYED TO MCSO A WATERSPOUT OCCURING ONE MILE SOUTH OF LONG KEY.

A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC REPORTED A LARGE WATERSPOUT NEAR EAST WASHERWOMAN SHOAL LIGHT MOVING EAST. THE WATERSPOUT BEGAN AT 5 PM EDT AND WAS CONTINUING THROUGH 520 PM. A SECOND MUCH THINNER WATERSPOUT WITH A SPRAY RING VISIBLE WAS LOCATED ABOUT 2 MILES EAST FROM 515 PM ONGOING THROUGH 520 PM ALSO MOVING EAST. A THIRD WATERSPOUT...THIN WITH A SPRAY RING...WAS REPORTED AROUND 532 PM IN THE SAME LOCATION. THIS LAST WATERSPOUT WAS NEARLY STATIONARY. THESE WATERSPOUTS WERE ALL DISSIPATED AS OF 538 PM.

A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC REPORTED A LARGE WATERSPOUT NEAR EAST WASHERWOMAN SHOAL LIGHT MOVING TO THE EAST. THE WATERSPOUT BEGAN AROUND 500 PM EDT AND WAS CONTINUING THROUGH 520 PM. A SECOND MUCH THINNER WATERSPOUT WITH A SPRAY RING VISIBLE WAS LOCATED ABOUT 2 MILES EAST FROM 515 PM ONGOING THROUGH 520 PM ALSO MOVING TO THE EAST. A THIRD WATERSPOUT...ALSO THIN WITH A VISIBILE SPRAY RING...WAS REPORTED AROUND 532 PM IN THE SAME LOCATION. THIS LAST WATERSPOUT WAS NEARLY STATIONARY AND ONGOING.
A TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER REPORTED SEVERAL SMALL WATERSPOUTS OVER THE PAST HALF HOUR JUST NORTH OF CUDJOE KEY. THE MOST RECENT WATERSPOUT LASTED SIX MINUTES AS IT DRIFTED SLOWLY WEST-NORTHWESTWARD.

MULTIPLE REPORTS OF A WATERSPOUT APPROXIMATELY 3 MILES NORTH OF VACA CUT, MOVING WEST-SOUTHWEST AT AROUND 10 KNOTS. SEA SURFACE WAS NOT VISIBLE AT THE TIME OF REPORT. DURATION WAS APPROXIMATELY 10 MINUTES.

A SMALL WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED BY U.S. COAST GUARD SECTOR KEY WEST PERSONNEL OVER KEY WEST HARBOR. THE WATERSPOUT DEVELOPED BENEATH A BUILDING CUMULUS CLOUD LINE EMANATING WESTWARD OFF THE LOWER FLORIDA KEYS. A CONDENSATION FUNNEL WAS NOT VISIBLE FOR MUCH OF THE DISTANCE BETWEEN CLOUD BASE AND THE WATER. HOWEVER...A SPRAY RING WAS OBSERVED AT THE SEA SURFACE. DURATION 8 MINUTES.

A MATURE WATERSPOUT WITH A SPRAY RING WAS OBSERVED BY BOTH A TRAINED MARINE WEATHER SPOTTER AND A CHARTER BOAT CAPTAIN. THE WATERSPOUT DEVELOPED BENEATH A CUMULUS CLOUD LINE...JUST EAST OF A DEVELOPING RAIN SHOWER. THE SPOUT WAS REPORTED TO BE NARROW...WITH THE FUNNEL EXTENDING COMPLETELY FROM CLOUD BASE TO THE WATER. IN ADDITION...A SPRAY RING WAS OBSERVED AT THE BASE OF THE SPOUT AT THE SEA SURFACE.
THE PULASKI SHOAL C-MAN STATION MEASURED A 35 KNOT WIND GUST DURING A RECENT THUNDERSTORM.

properties.city: 1 W EAST END OF LONG KE
properties.remark: A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED A WATERSPOUT ABOUT A HALF-MILE NORTH OF THE LONG KEY BRIDGE. IT WAS VERTICAL AND NEARLY STATIONARY WITH A VISIBLE SPRAY RING. DURATION WAS ABOUT 7 MINUTES BEFORE BEING OVERTAKEN BY A NEARBY SHOWER.

WATERSPOUT OBSERVED JUST SOUTH OF STOCK ISLAND. FUNNEL CLOUD EXTENDED APPROXIMATELY ONE QUARTER OF THE WAY DOWN FROM CLOUD BASE. MOVEMENT WAS SLOWLY WEST-SOUTHWEST. INTERMITTENT DURATION OF 17 MINUTES...DISSIPATED AT 1210 PM EDT DUE TO A NEARBY THUNDERSTORM.

MULTIPLE SOCIAL MEDIA REPORTS OF A MATURE WATERSPOUT GULFSIDE OF THE CHANNEL 5 BRIDGE NEAR LONG KEY. AERIAL PHOTOGRAPHY SHOWED A CONDENSATION FUNNEL EXTENDING FROM THE CLOUD BASE TO THE SEA SURFACE WITH A VISIBLE SPRAY RING. DURATION OF THE WATERSPOUT IS UNKNOWN.

MULTIPLE SOCIAL MEDIA REPORTS OF A MATURE WATERSPOUT GULFSIDE OF THE CHANNEL 5 BRIDGE NEAR LONG KEY. AERIAL PHOTOGRAPHY SHOWS A VISIBLE SPRAY RING. DURATION IS UNKNOWN AND THE TIME OF OCCURRENCE IS ESTIMATED TO BE SHORTLY AFTER 2PM.

AN OFF-DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED A WATERSPOUT TO THE DISTANT NORTHEAST OF BIG PINE KEY. THE CONDENSATION FUNNEL EXTENDED HALFWAY DOWN FROM THE CLOUD BASE. A SPRAY RING AT THE SEA SURFACE WAS NOT VISIBLE DUE TO DISTANCE FROM THE WATERSPOUT. DURATION WAS 10 MINUTES.
AN OFF-DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED A SHORT-LIVED WATERSPOUT JUST SOUTH OF KEY WEST AT 1154 AM EDT. DURATION WAS APPROXIMATELY 2 MINUTES.

A WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED FROM THE PUBLIC NORTH OF KEY WEST VIA SOCIAL MEDIA. NWS METEOROLOGIST IN KEY WEST ALSO OBSERVED MULTIPLE WATERSPOUTS NORTH TO NORTHWEST OF THE CITY. WATERSPOUT CHARACTERISTICS RANGED FROM SKINNY TO MEDIUM THICKNESS, VARYING STRAIGHT UP AND DOWN TO TILTED GREATER THAN 30 DEGREES. SEAS SURFACE WAS NOT VISIBLE, AND MOVEMENT WAS TO THE WEST SOUTHWEST. AVERAGE DURATION WAS BETWEEN 15 TO 20 MINUTES.
AN OFF DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED A WATERSPOUT ABOUT 5 TO 6 MILES SOUTH OF SMATHERS BEACH THAT LASTED 7 OR 8 MINUTES.

A MARINE THUNDERSTORM PRODUCED AN E WIND GUST OF 40 KNOTS...46 MPH WAS MEASURED BY THE WEATHERFLOW MESONET SITE...ID XALG...LOCATED AT ALLIGATOR REEF LIGHT AT 1110 AM EDT. SUSTAINED WINDS WERE 27 KNOTS...31 MPH.

A MARINE THUNDERSTORM PRODUCED AN E WIND GUST OF 35 KNOTS...OR 40 MPH WAS MEASURED BY THE WEATHERFLOW MESONET SITE...ID XALG...LOCATED AT ALLIGATOR REEF LIGHT AT 1115 AM EDT. WINDS WERE SUSTAINED AT 28 KNOTS.. OR 32 MPH.
A MARINE THUNDERSTORM GENERATED A SOUTH WIND GUST OF 37 KNOTS...OR 43 MPH WAS MEASURED BY THE MESONET STATION LOCATED ON LOWER MATECUMBE KEY.
A WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED VIA SOCIAL MEDIA NEAR ISLAMORADA...DURATION UNKNOWN...LOCATION ESTIMATED ABOUT 2 MILES OFFSHORE WITH SPRAY RING CLEARLY VISIBLE.
A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC OBSERVED A WELL DEVELOPED WATERSPOUT 12 MILES SOUTH OF TEA TABLE KEY WITH SPRAY RING VISIBLE...WATERSPOUT LASTED 4 MINUTES.
A BOAT CAPTAIN OBSERVED A WELL DEVELOPED WATERSPOUT 11 MILES SOUTHEAST OF WINDLEY KEY WITH A LARGE SPRAY RING VISIBLE...WATERSPOUT LASTED 5 TO 7 MINUTES...REPORTED VIA SOCIAL MEDIA.
A MARINE THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS...OR 39 MPH WAS MEASURED BY THE MESONET STATION LOCATED ON LONG KEY.

A MARINE THUNDERSTORM PRODUCED A WIND GUST OF 43 KNOTS...OR 49 MPH WAS MEASURED BY THE WEATHERFLOW MESONET STATION LOCATED ON CARYSFORT REEF LIGHT AT 148 PM EDT. SUSTAINED WINDS WERE 34 KNOTS...OR 39 MPH.

A TRAINED MARINE WEATHER SPOTTER REPORTED A WATERSPOUT OCEANSIDE OF KEY LARGO, OFF OF EL RADABOB KEY. THIS WAS THE SECOND VIEWED WATERSPOUT IN THE AREA AND THE CONDENSATION FUNNEL BEGAN TO FURTHER EXTEND DOWNWARD FROM THE CLOUD BASE. SEA SURFACE WAS NOT VISIBLE.
A WIND GUST OF 36 KNOTS...42 MPH...FROM THE EAST WAS MEASURED AT AN AUTOMATED WEATHERFLOW STATION AT SMITH SHOAL LIGHT DURING THE PASSAGE OF A THUNDERSTORM.
